skill.name: task-estimation
skill.description: Estimate software development tasks accurately using various techniques. Use when planning sprints, roadmaps, or project timelines. Handles story points, t-shirt sizing, planning poker, and estimation best practices.
skill.summary: Estimate agile software work with story points, t-shirt sizes, planning poker, and risk buffers.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged markdown fences, inline backticks, external reference links, and weak-crypto patterns. Manual review found no executable commands, no cryptographic code, no automatic network access, and no prompt injection attempt. The skill is documentation-only agile estimation guidance and is safe to publish.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Blocks Misidentified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution across markdown examples. The locations are fenced markdown, plain example text, and inline tag formatting, not executable skill code. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are documentation fences or inline markdown syntax. No executable script file, shell invocation, or user-controlled command path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Not Present
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns at descriptive text and template lines. Manual review found estimation content only, with no MD5, SHA-1, encryption, hashing, or credential handling code. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain plain task-estimation prose or references. There is no cryptographic operation or security-sensitive data flow to evaluate.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reference Links Are Not Network Behavior
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged hardcoded URLs in the references section. These are educational links to Scrum Guide, Planning Poker, and Atlassian pages; the skill does not instruct an agent to fetch them or send data to them. Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only as markdown reference links. No automated request, webhook, credential transfer, or exfiltration behavior is present.

content.value_statement: Software teams often struggle to compare task complexity without turning estimates into commitments. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code create structured estimates with story points, t-shirt sizes, risk notes, and dependencies.
actual_capabilities: Maps software tasks to Fibonacci story point ranges.
Converts rough work items into t-shirt sizes for early planning.
Guides planning poker discussions and consensus review.
Adjusts estimates for risk, uncertainty, and dependencies.
Formats estimates with acceptance criteria, breakdowns, risks, and notes.
limitations: Does not predict exact delivery dates or guarantee completion time.
Requires clear task scope and acceptance criteria for useful estimates.
Depends on team velocity and historical examples for calibration.
Does not replace team discussion during planning poker.

use_cases.title: Prepare Sprint Planning
use_cases.description: Estimate backlog items before a sprint planning meeting and identify stories that need splitting.

use_cases.title: Size Product Roadmaps
use_cases.description: Group proposed features into rough sizes so roadmap options can be compared early.

use_cases.title: Review Engineering Scope
use_cases.description: Break a technical task into frontend, backend, testing, risk, and dependency components.

prompt_templates.title: Estimate One Task
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner estimate for a single backlog item.
prompt_templates.prompt: Estimate this task using story points, t-shirt size, estimated time range, risks, and dependencies: [paste task].

prompt_templates.title: Split Large Story
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review for a story that may be too large.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this story for estimation. If it is 13 points or larger, split it into smaller stories with separate estimates: [paste story].

prompt_templates.title: Run Planning Poker Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Team discussion support after different estimates are proposed.
prompt_templates.prompt: Act as a planning poker facilitator. Compare these estimates, list the likely reasons for disagreement, and propose a consensus path: [paste votes and notes].

prompt_templates.title: Estimate a Sprint Backlog
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning for multiple tasks and capacity review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Estimate this sprint backlog using story points, t-shirt sizes, risks, dependencies, and splitting recommendations. Use our velocity of [number] points: [paste backlog].

output_examples.input: Estimate a profile photo upload feature with file validation and existing cloud storage.
output_examples.output: Story Points: 5. T-Shirt Size: M. Estimated Time: 1-2 days. Breakdown: UI upload control, validation, storage integration, and tests. Risks: image processing edge cases and file size limits.

output_examples.input: Estimate a payment provider integration with checkout, webhook handling, and refunds.
output_examples.output: Story Points: 13. T-Shirt Size: XL. Recommendation: split into checkout, webhook processing, refund flow, and test environment setup. Risks: external provider behavior, security review, and failure handling.

output_examples.input: Compare planning poker votes of 3, 5, and 8 for an admin export feature.
output_examples.output: Consensus review should focus on data volume, permission checks, export format, and test coverage. A 5 point estimate is reasonable if export infrastructure already exists.

best_practices: Use recent completed work as reference stories before assigning points.
Document assumptions, risks, and dependencies beside each estimate.
Split work that reaches 13 points or has unclear acceptance criteria.
anti_patterns: Do not treat estimates as individual performance commitments.
Do not force precise hour estimates when scope is still uncertain.
Do not average planning poker votes without discussing high and low estimates.

faq.question: Does this skill create exact delivery dates?
faq.answer: No. It provides relative estimates and planning ranges, not guaranteed dates.

faq.question: Which estimation methods does it support?
faq.answer: It supports story points, t-shirt sizing, planning poker, risk buffers, and task breakdowns.

faq.question: Can it estimate a full sprint backlog?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the backlog, team velocity, known dependencies, and any historical reference stories.

faq.question: How should teams handle 13 point stories?
faq.answer: The skill recommends splitting 13 point or larger stories into smaller, clearer work items.

faq.question: Does it replace team consensus?
faq.answer: No. It helps structure the discussion, but the team should agree on final estimates.

faq.question: Can it account for uncertainty?
faq.answer: Yes. It asks for risk and uncertainty factors, then adds a buffer to the base estimate.
